description: A clean MIT fork of Git Graph. Visual history, branch actions, and devcontainer support.
config.title: Git Graph Alter
config.autoCenterCommitDetailsView: Automatically center the commit details view when it is opened.
config.commitDetailsView.location: Specifies where the Commit Details View is displayed: inline within the graph, or docked to the bottom of the window.
config.referenceLabels.alignment: Specifies how branch and tag labels are aligned on each commit in the graph.
config.referenceLabels.combineLocalAndRe: Combine a local branch and its remote-tracking branch into a single label when they point at the same commit.
config.commitOrder: Specifies the order in which commits are displayed on the Git Graph View. See git log --help for details on each ordering.
config.commits.showSignatureStatus: Show the signature-verification status (a tick / question mark / cross) of signed commits on the Git Graph View. Verifying signatures is comparatively slow. Default: false
config.contextMenuActionsVisibility: Customise which actions are visible on the Git Graph View context menus. Each action defaults to visible; set it to false to hide it.
config.contextMenuActionsVisibility.comm: Visibility of the actions on the file context menu in the Commit Details View.
config.customEmojiShortcodeMappings: An array of custom shortcode-to-emoji mappings used to replace emoji shortcodes (e.g. :rocket:) in commit messages. These take precedence over the built-in mappings.
config.customBranchGlobPatterns: Custom glob patterns shown as selectable entries in the Branches dropdown; selecting one shows only the branches whose name matches the glob.
config.customBranchGlobPatterns.name: The label shown in the Branches dropdown.
config.customBranchGlobPatterns.glob: The glob pattern matched against branch names (e.g. feature/*).
config.customEmojiShortcodeMappings.shor: The emoji shortcode, with or without surrounding colons (e.g. :rocket: or rocket).
config.customEmojiShortcodeMappings.emoj: The emoji to display in place of the shortcode (e.g. 🚀).
config.commitOrder.date: Show commits in reverse-chronological order by commit date (git log --date-order)
config.commitOrder.authorDate: Show commits in reverse-chronological order by author date (git log --author-date-order)
config.commitOrder.topo: Show commits in topological order, avoiding intermixing of parallel branches (git log --topo-order)
config.dateFormat: Specifies the date format to be used in the date column of Git Graph.
config.dateFormat.dateTime: Show the date and time, for example "19 Mar 2019 21:34"
config.dateFormat.dateOnly: Show the date only, for example "19 Mar 2019"
config.dateFormat.relative: Show relative times, for example "5 minutes ago"
config.dateType: Specifies the date type to be displayed throughout Git Graph.
config.defaultColumnVisibility: The initial visibility of the Date, Author and Commit columns on the Git Graph View; columns can also be toggled by right-clicking a column header. Default: all visible
config.enhancedAccessibility: Visual file change indicators (A/M/D/R/U) are shown in the Commit Details View so file change types are not conveyed by colour alone. Default: false
config.dateType.authorDate: Use the author date of a commit
config.dateType.commitDate: Use the committer date of a commit
config.fetchAvatars: Fetch avatars of commit authors and committers. By enabling this setting, you consent to commit author and committer email addresses being sent GitHub, GitLab or Gravatar, depending on the repositories remote origin.
config.autoFetchEnabled: Periodically fetch from all remotes in the background and refresh the graph. Default: false
config.autoFetchIntervalMinutes: How often auto-fetch runs, in minutes (1–60). Default: 10
config.fetchAndPrune: Prune deleted remote-tracking references when fetching from remote(s) (git fetch --prune). Default: false
config.fetchAndPruneTags: Also prune local tags that no longer exist on the remote when fetching with pruning (git fetch --prune --prune-tags). Default: false
config.fileTree.compactFolders: Compact the file tree in the Commit Details View by combining folders that contain a single subfolder into a single row. Default: true
config.fileView.type: How the changed files are displayed in the Commit Details View: "File Tree" (nested folders) or "File List" (a flat, path-sorted list). Default: File Tree
config.graphColours: Specifies the colours used on the graph.
config.graphStyle: Specifies the style of the graph.
config.graphStyle.rounded: Use smooth curves when transitioning between branches on the graph
config.graphStyle.angular: Use angular lines when transitioning between branches on the graph
config.includeCommitsMentionedByReflogs: Include commits that are only mentioned by reflogs (e.g. orphaned by a reset or rebase) on the Git Graph View. Default: false
config.initialLoadCommits: Specifies the number of commits to initially load.
config.loadMoreCommits: Specifies the number of commits to load when the "Load More Commits" button is pressed (only shown when more commits are available).
config.loadMoreAutomatically: Automatically load the next page of commits when you scroll to the bottom of the Git Graph View, instead of clicking "Load More Commits". Default: false
config.maxDepthOfRepoSearch: Specifies the maximum depth of subfolders to search when discovering repositories in the workspace.
config.markdown: Render a subset of inline Markdown (bold, italic, inline code, strikethrough) in commit and tag messages. Default: true
config.mute.commitsThatAreNotAncestorsOf: Display commits that are not ancestors of the checked out commit (HEAD) with a muted text colour on the Git Graph View. Default: false (disabled)
config.muteMergeCommits: Display merge commits with a muted text colour on the Git Graph View. Default: true (enabled)
config.onLoad.scrollToHead: Automatically scroll the Git Graph View to the commit referenced by HEAD when it is opened. Default: false
config.onlyFollowFirstParent: Only follow the first parent of commits when discovering the commits to load on the Git Graph View (git log --first-parent). Default: false
config.openToTheRepoOfTheActiveTextEdito: When opening the Git Graph View, automatically select the repository containing the active text editor's file. Default: false
config.openNewTabEditorGroup: Specifies the editor group where the Visual Studio Code Diff View opens when viewing a file change: Active, Beside, or a specific group One through Nine.
config.retainContextWhenHidden: Keep the Git Graph view's content loaded when it is hidden (e.g. another tab is focused), so it restores almost instantly. Uses more memory while running in the background. Default: false
config.referenceInputSpaceSubstitution: Automatically substitute spaces typed into branch/tag name inputs (git references can't contain spaces). "None" leaves them as-is. Default: None
config.showCommitsOnlyReferencedByTags: Show commits that are solely referenced by tags (i.e. not reachable from any branch) when all branches are shown. Default: true
config.showCurrentBranchByDefault: Show the current branch by default when Git Graph is opened. Default: false (show all branches)
config.repository.sign.commits: Sign commits created by Git Graph View actions (merge, cherry pick, revert, rebase, squash). An alternative to git's commit.gpgSign config. Default: false
config.repository.sign.tags: Sign annotated tags created by Git Graph View actions. An alternative to git's tag.gpgSign config. Default: false
config.showRemoteBranches: Show remote branches by default when the Git Graph View is opened. Default: true
config.showRemoteHeads: Show remote HEAD symbolic references (e.g. origin/HEAD) on the Git Graph View. Default: true
config.sourceCodeProviderIntegrationLoca: Specifies where the Git Graph menu item appears on the title of a Source Control Provider in the Source Control View.
Show inline on the Source Control Provider title
Show on the 'More actions...' menu of the Source Control Provider
config.showStatusBarItem: Show a Status Bar item which opens Git Graph when clicked.
config.showUncommittedChanges: Show uncommitted changes (set to false to decrease load time on large repositories).
config.tabIconColourTheme: Specifies the colour theme of the icon displayed on the Git Graph tab.
config.tabIconColourTheme.colour: Show a colour icon which suits most Visual Studio Code colour themes
config.tabIconColourTheme.grey: Show a grey icon which suits Visual Studio Code colour themes that are predominantly grayscale
config.showTags: Show tags on the Git Graph View. Default: true (enabled)
config.showStashes: Show stashes on the Git Graph View (as nodes branching off the commit they were created on). Default: true
config.showUntrackedFiles: Show untracked files in the uncommitted changes on the Git Graph View. Default: true (enabled)
config.dateFormat.isoDateTime: Show the ISO date and time, for example "2019-03-19 21:34:00"
config.dateFormat.isoDateOnly: Show the ISO date only, for example "2019-03-19"
config.useMailmap: Use .mailmap files when displaying author and committer names and email addresses. Default: false
config.fileEncoding: The encoding used to decode file contents shown in the Diff View and when viewing a file at a revision (any encoding supported by iconv-lite). Default: utf8
config.showSpecificBranches: Branch names initially shown in the graph when a repository is first opened (combined with the checked-out branch when that option is enabled). Empty shows all branches.
config.graph.uncommittedChanges: Where to draw the open circle for the Uncommitted Changes on the graph.
config.issueLinkingRegex: A regular expression matching issue numbers in commit messages (e.g. "#(\\d+)"). Capture groups can be referenced in issueLinkingUrl as $1, $2, etc. Leave empty to disable issue linking.
config.issueLinkingUrl: The URL template that issue references link to, with $1, $2, ... substituted from the issueLinkingRegex capture groups (e.g. "https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/$1").
config.keyboardShortcut.find: The keybinding for the keyboard shortcut that opens the Find Widget in the Git Graph View. Set to "UNASSIGNED" to disable it.
config.keyboardShortcut.refresh: The keybinding for the keyboard shortcut that refreshes the Git Graph View. Set to "UNASSIGNED" to disable it.
config.keyboardShortcut.scrollToHead: The keybinding for the keyboard shortcut that scrolls the Git Graph View to the commit referenced by HEAD. Set to "UNASSIGNED" to disable it.
config.keyboardShortcut.scrollToStash: The keybinding for the keyboard shortcut that scrolls the Git Graph View to the next stash (or the previous stash when Shift is held). Set to "UNASSIGNED" to disable it.
config.dialog.deleteBranch.forceDelete: Default value of the "Force Delete" checkbox on the Delete Branch dialog. Default: false
config.dialog.cherryPick.noCommit: Default value of the "No Commit" checkbox on the Cherry Pick dialog. Default: false
config.dialog.addTag.type: Default tag type selected on the Add Tag dialog (annotated or lightweight).
config.dialog.createBranch.checkOut: Default value of the "Check out" checkbox on the Create Branch dialog. Default: false
config.dialog.merge.noFastForward: Default value of the "Create a new commit even if fast-forward is possible" checkbox on the Merge dialog. Default: true
config.dialog.merge.squash: Default value of the "Squash commits" checkbox on the Merge dialog. Default: false
config.dialog.merge.squashMessageFormat: Specifies the message format of the squash commit created when squashing via the Merge dialog.
Use the squash commit message generated by Git Graph (e.g. "Merge branch 'x'")
Use the detailed squash commit message generated by Git (the list of squashed commits stored in .git/SQUASH_MSG)
config.dialog.resetCurrentBranch.mode: Default reset mode selected on the Reset Current Branch to Commit dialog (soft, mixed, or hard).
config.deprecatedRenamed: This setting has been renamed and grouped. It is still read for backward compatibility — please migrate to the new grouped setting.
config.followSourceControlSelection: When enabled, Git Graph follows the repository selected in the Source Control view, opening or switching the graph to it. When several repositories are selected, a repository dropdown in the Graph control bar lets you choose which one to display. Default: enabled
